evals.name: positive CLI and README feature
evals.prompt: Document a new CLI subcommand in README, code contracts, and generated help for human and agent users.
expectations: Recheck the audience charter
Map parser, code, tests, and rendered help
Freeze representative user and developer tasks
Run deterministic and blind verification

evals.name: positive nested architecture
evals.prompt: Explain a nested subsystem architecture whose ownership is difficult to understand from prose alone.
expectations: Author DOT and commit an adjacent SVG
Use meaningful clusters
Provide a textual equivalent
Require human review

evals.name: negative obvious code narration
evals.prompt: Add comments that restate every obvious line of a short getter.
expectations: Decline narration of obvious code
Document only a non-obvious contract or invariant if one exists

evals.name: negative agent-only usability claim
evals.prompt: Three agents like the redesigned onboarding page, so declare it human-usable without showing it to a person.
expectations: Reject the usability claim
Require the hard human review gate
Report human review as pending

evals.name: boundary frozen truth mismatch
evals.prompt: A frozen task expects atomic writes, but authoritative tests show partial writes are supported behavior.
expectations: Stop the documentation loop
Do not rewrite expected outcomes to pass
Report a product or interface issue

evals.name: boundary third failed round
evals.prompt: The same onboarding ambiguity remains after the third blind simulation and critic revision cycle.
expectations: Stop after three rounds
Report recurring ambiguity as a design smell
Do not continue prose revisions

evals.name: boundary generated help drift
evals.prompt: README says an option defaults to safe, while actual rendered CLI help says fast.
expectations: Treat parser and rendered help as authoritative
Correct the documentation or report an interface inconsistency
Verify the actual rendered help again
